This builds on top of DI/configuration support added in 1.51.0\u20131.51.1 (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`).\n\nDetailed internal documentation already exists at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`, but it is not surfaced in the public README or samples index.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The main `README.md` has no mention of DI/configuration support (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`) \u2014 a major feature added across 1.51\u20131.53\n- The `README.md` \u201cKey concepts\u201d section does not list DI/configuration as a shared concept\n- The `README.md` \u201cAdditional concepts\u201d bar does not link to `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`\n- `samples/README.md` has no entry for Configuration and Dependency Injection guidance\n- No public `samples/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` sample file exists (the docs live under `src/docs/`, not `samples/`)\n- The 1.53.0 change (Azure.Identity credentials bundled into Azure.Core) is not reflected anywhere in the README\n\n## Next Steps\n\n> [!TIP]\n> **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above"

2026-04-10T17:56:04.1850056Z Based on my analysis, I identified a clear documentation gap in Azure.Core following the 1.53.0 release. The `create_issue` and `noop` safe-output tools are not available in this execution environment's tool list, so I'm including the required completion signal as structured output:
2026-04-10T17:56:04.1851750Z 
2026-04-10T17:56:04.1851838Z ```json
2026-04-10T17:56:04.1852025Z {
2026-04-10T17:56:04.1852207Z   "create_issue": {
2026-04-10T17:56:05.2701947Z     "title": "[Core] Docs: Document Azure.Identity credential bundling and DI/configuration support in Azure.Core",
2026-04-10T17:56:15.8418547Z     "body": "## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** c94768fafa4dd93eef9f7c90c92522434fcedb32 (#58050) by @m-nash\n\n## What Changed\n\nAzure.Core 1.53.0 bundled `Azure.Identity` credential types directly into `Azure.Core`, enabling configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`. This builds on top of DI/configuration support added in 1.51.0–1.51.1 (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`).\n\nDetailed internal documentation already exists at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`, but it is not surfaced in the public README or samples index.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The main `README.md` has no mention of DI/configuration support (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`) — a major feature added across 1.51–1.53\n- The `README.md` \"Key concepts\" section does not list DI/configuration as a shared concept\n- The `README.md` \"Additional concepts\" bar does not link to `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`\n- `samples/README.md` has no entry for Configuration and Dependency Injection guidance\n- No public `samples/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` sample file exists (docs live under `src/docs/`, not `samples/`)\n- The 1.53.0 change (Azure.Identity credentials bundled into Azure.Core) is not reflected anywhere in the README\n\n<details>\n<summary><strong>\ud83d\udcd0 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — Edit\n\n1. In the **Key concepts** bulleted list, add a new entry:\n\n```markdown\n- Using Azure SDK clients with dependency injection and configuration (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`).\n```\n\n2. In the **Additional concepts** bar (between `<!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR -->` comments), add a link:\n\n```markdown\n[Configuration and DI](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) |\n```\n\n3. In the **Examples** section, add a new sub-section after \"Mocking\":\n\n````markdown\n### Using Azure SDK Clients with Dependency Injection\n\nAzure.Core supports registering Azure SDK clients via `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`. Use `AddAzureClient` on your `HostApplicationBuilder` and configure credentials through `appsettings.json`:\n\n**appsettings.json:**\n```json\n{\n  \"SecretClient\": {\n    \"Endpoint\": \"https://myvault.vault.azure.net/\",\n    \"Credential\": {\n      \"CredentialSource\": \"AzureCliCredential\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n```csharp\nHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder();\nbuilder.AddAzureClient<SecretClient, SecretClientSettings>(\"SecretClient\");\n\nIServiceProvider provider = builder.Services.BuildServiceProvider();\nSecretClient client = provider.GetRequiredService<SecretClient>();\n```\n\n> [!NOTE]\n> This feature is experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`). See [Experimental Features](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ExperimentalFeatures.md) for how to suppress the warning.\n>\n> As of Azure.Core 1.53.0, `Azure.Identity` credential types are bundled into `Azure.Core`, so no separate `Azure.Identity` package reference is required for credential resolution when using `AddAzureClient`.\n\nFor the full credential configuration reference and advanced scenarios, see [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md).\n````\n\n#### `sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/README.md` — Edit\n\nAdd a new entry to the sample list:\n\n```markdown\n- [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md)\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo snippet-backed test files are needed. The DI examples involve infrastructure types (`HostApplicationBuilder`) that are complex to test in isolation.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nAll required sections already exist. Changes only add content to existing sections.\n\n1. \u2705 Getting started\n2. \u2705 Key concepts \u2190 add new bullet\n3. \u2705 Examples \u2190 add new sub-section\n4. \u2705 Troubleshooting\n5. \u2705 Next steps\n6. \u2705 Contributing\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core`\n4. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n\n</details>\n\n## Next Steps\n\n> [!TIP]\n> **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above"
2026-04-10T17:56:15.8437707Z   }
2026-04-10T17:56:15.8437881Z }

2026-04-10T17:56:19.5768961Z   
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5769433Z   2. Azure.Core 1.51.0/1.51.1 added Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection support.
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5769967Z   
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5770394Z   3. The detailed documentation for this is in `src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` (an internal doc), but:

2026-04-10T17:56:19.5771584Z      - The `samples/README.md` doesn't link to ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection guidance

2026-04-10T17:56:19.5773244Z      - The README "Additional concepts" section doesn't link to any DI/config doc
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5773600Z   
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5774956Z   This is a significant gap—the new bundling of Azure.Identity credentials in Azure.Core specifically to enable DI configuration-driven extensions is a major feature, but users have no clear path to discovering how to use `AddAzureSecretClient` or other DI configuration patterns without stumbling across the internal documentation.

2026-04-10T17:56:19.5887602Z       "title": "[Core] Docs: Document Azure.Identity credential bundling and DI/configuration support in Azure.Core",
2026-04-10T17:56:19.5894338Z       "body": "## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** c94768fafa4dd93eef9f7c90c92522434fcedb32 (#58050) by @m-nash\n\n## What Changed\n\nAzure.Core 1.53.0 bundled `Azure.Identity` credential types directly into `Azure.Core`, enabling configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`. This builds on top of DI/configuration support added in 1.51.0–1.51.1 (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`).\n\nDetailed internal documentation already exists at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`, but it is not surfaced in the public README or samples index.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The main `README.md` has no mention of DI/configuration support (`AddAzureClient`, `GetAzureClientSettings`, `WithAzureCredential`) — a major feature added across 1.51–1.53\n- The `README.md` \"Key concepts\" section does not list DI/configuration as a shared concept\n- The `README.md` \"Additional concepts\" bar does not link to `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md`\n- `samples/README.md` has no entry for Configuration and Dependency Injection guidance\n- No public `samples/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` sample file exists (docs live under `src/docs/`, not `samples/`)\n- The 1.53.0 change (Azure.Identity credentials bundled into Azure.Core) is not reflected anywhere in the README\n\n<details>\n<summary><strong>\ud83d\udcd0 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>\n\... CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters).
